title: Rootless podman and slirp4netns slow performance with NGINX (~5 second reponse)
body:
### Issue Description

NGINX has poor performance when running under rootless podman and slirp4netns

Lowering the MTU to 1500 from the podman default of 65520 with `--network slirp4netns:mtu=1500` fixes the issue. Strangely, after I ran the container once with the `--network slirp4netns:mtu=1500` option, NGINX container works fine even with the option removed.

Please see #6945  for more details, I'm having exactly the same issue mentioned there.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

```
 git clone https://github.com/arbrsts/nginx-rootless-podman-poor-performance-reproducible-example
 podman build ./nginx/. -t slow-load
 podman run -p 4000:4000 slow-load
 npm i
 npm run dev
```

### Describe the results you received

Page load takes 5 seconds.

### Describe the results you expected

Page load should be quick.

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

~~No~~
Tried again with latest Fedora release (4.7.0) and issue persists

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

~~I wanted to try and reproduce this issue on the latest podman release (4.7.0 for Fedora) however I can't reproduce the issue any more even after removing `--network slirp4netns:mtu=1500` option.~~

Issue persists with the latest Fedora release of Podman (4.7.0)

comments.body: does it work any better if you use pasta with `--network pasta`?
I'm trying to get it to work with pasta but struggling to access the host network, I tried adding pasta:--map-gw option.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@rhatdan I've just resorted to using host network option for podman
pasta is now the default and I going to assume it works better there
